nodes.title: Validate package input first
nodes.why: A clear early error is easier to debug than weird behavior later.
nodes.alternative: Try to keep going with invalid input.
nodes.tradeoff: Strict validation is safer, but callers must pass valid data.

nodes.title: Choose import vs require mode
nodes.why: Packages often expose different entry files for ESM and CommonJS.
nodes.tradeoff: More accurate resolution, with a tiny bit more branching.

nodes.title: Build a deterministic condition priority list
nodes.why: This keeps resolution predictable and removes duplicate conditions.
nodes.tradeoff: Stable results, but behavior now depends on this explicit priority.

nodes.title: Recursively walk conditional exports
nodes.why: Real package exports can be nested, so one-level checks are not enough.
nodes.tradeoff: Handles more real-world cases, with slightly more logic.

nodes.title: Support exports root at `.`
nodes.why: Many packages define the main entry under `exports['.']`.
nodes.alternative: Assume the whole exports object is already the root target.
nodes.tradeoff: Better compatibility with package conventions, with one extra check.

nodes.title: Use module/main fallback when exports fails
nodes.why: This keeps behavior useful for older or simpler package metadata.
nodes.alternative: Throw immediately when exports is missing or unmatched.
nodes.tradeoff: More forgiving behavior, but fallback can hide incomplete metadata.

nodes.title: Throw if nothing resolves
nodes.why: Failing loudly prevents silent bundling with the wrong file.
nodes.tradeoff: Stronger safety signal, but callers must handle the exception.
